---
title: "Quarto 2 — Code Options and Tables"
subtitle: "One reliable table first; advanced methods second"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
institute: "NIEHS"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, niehs-theme.scss]
    logo: ../../images/niehs-logo.png
    footer: "R Markdown + Quarto · NIEHS · 2026"
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## The required outcome — 60 minutes

> Add one captioned `knitr::kable()` table to `student_work/my_report.qmd` and render it from a clean session.

::: {.fragment}
`kableExtra`, `DT`, `broom`, and mixed R/Python are demonstrations or bonus material.
:::

# Execution options {.section-divider}

## Quarto form

```` markdown
```{r}`r ''`
#| label: tbl-site-summary
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| tbl-cap: "Synthetic biomarker summary by site."

# code
```
````

::: {.fragment}
Use unique labels. A table label begins `tbl-`.
:::

## Cache and freeze are different

::: {.incremental}
- `cache: true` uses the active execution engine's cache.
- Project `freeze` reuses completed document execution results during project renders.
- Neither replaces a final clean-session validation.
:::

# Tables {.section-divider}

## The portable baseline

```r
site_summary |>
  knitr::kable(
    digits = 2,
    col.names = c("Site", "N", "Mean PFOA")
  )
```

::: {.fragment}
Put the cross-reference caption in `#| tbl-cap`, not in both places.
:::

## Advanced choices

| Method | Use | Portability |
|---|---|---|
| `kable()` | Simple report table | Strong |
| `kableExtra` | Format-specific styling | Test every output |
| `DT` | Searchable HTML table | HTML only; provide a static fallback |
| `broom` | Model objects → data frames | Pair with a table method |

## HTML widget fallback

```r
if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  DT::datatable(my_data)
} else {
  knitr::kable(my_data)
}
```

# Mixed language {.section-divider}

## Demonstration only

A document containing R and Python uses knitr with `reticulate` for the Python chunks. That adds an environment dependency.

::: {.callout-warning}
Do not make Python setup a prerequisite for the core workshop.
:::

# Hands-on {.section-divider}

## Add the required table

In `student_work/my_report.qmd`:

1. Summarize record count and mean PFOA by site.
2. Display the result with `knitr::kable()`.
3. Add `tbl-site-summary` and an informative `tbl-cap`.
4. Render from a restarted R session.

Recovery: `student_work/checkpoints/02_table_complete.qmd`.
